activities_projects.title: Simulating Blood Filtration
activities_projects.objective: Understand how kidneys filter waste from the blood
materials: Mixture of water, sand, and red food color
steps: Prepare a 'mock blood' solution using water, red dye (plasma/RBCs), and sand (waste)
Pour the mixture through the filter paper into a beaker
Observe what stays on the paper and what passes through
Compare this to the function of nephrons in the human kidney
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Explains the selective filtration process of the excretory system
activities_projects.evaluation: Logical comparison between the model and real kidney function (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Oxygen-Carbon Dioxide Exchange
activities_projects.objective: Compare the composition of inhaled and exhaled air
steps: Use a syringe to pump atmospheric air into Jar A containing lime water
Exhale through a tube into Jar B containing lime water
Time how long it takes for the lime water to turn milky in each jar
Analyze why exhaled air reacts faster
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Quantifies the higher concentration of $CO_2$ in exhaled air
activities_projects.evaluation: Accurate recording of time and correct scientific reasoning (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Heart Rate Recovery Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Measure the time taken for the heart to return to its resting rate
steps: Measure your resting heart rate (Pulse A)
Perform 50 jumping jacks and measure pulse immediately after (Pulse B)
Measure pulse every minute until it returns to Pulse A
Plot a graph of 'Time vs. Heart Rate'
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands cardiovascular efficiency and recovery time
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct data plotting and analysis of physical fitness levels (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Dialysis Mechanism Study
activities_projects.objective: Learn about the concept of artificial kidneys
steps: Tie salt water inside a cellophane bag (semi-permeable membrane)
Place the bag in a beaker of pure water
After an hour, test the water in the beaker for saltiness
Relate this to how a dialysis machine removes urea from blood
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Gains insight into the principle of osmosis and dialysis
activities_projects.evaluation: Clear explanation of how waste moves through a membrane (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Mapping the Circulatory Route
activities_projects.objective: Trace the flow of oxygenated and deoxygenated blood
steps: Draw a schematic diagram of the human heart with four chambers
Use Red arrows to show oxygen-rich blood from lungs to body
Use Blue arrows to show carbon dioxide-rich blood from body to lungs
Label the Pulmonary Artery and Pulmonary Vein correctly
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Distinguishes between systemic and pulmonary circulation
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct directional flow and anatomical labeling (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Starch Digestion by Saliva
activities_projects.objective: Observe the chemical change in food within the mouth
steps: Place plain boiled rice in Tube A
Chew some rice for 3 minutes and place it in Tube B
Add iodine solution to both
Observe why Tube B does not turn blue-black while Tube A does
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Demonstrates the action of salivary amylase on starch
activities_projects.evaluation: Scientific explanation of the disappearance of starch (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Capillary Action Demonstration
activities_projects.objective: Understand how blood reaches tiny tissues
steps: Touch the tip of a narrow tube to the surface of colored water
Observe the water rising up the tube automatically
Discuss how this relates to blood flow in the smallest blood vessels
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Relates the physical property of capillary action to the circulatory system
activities_projects.evaluation: Observations on height of liquid vs. tube diameter (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Anaerobic Respiration in Yeast
activities_projects.objective: Compare human respiration with microbial respiration
steps: Mix yeast and sugar in a test tube with a little warm water
Fix a balloon over the mouth of the tube
Observe the balloon inflate and smell the contents after an hour
Discuss the production of alcohol and $CO_2$
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies the products of anaerobic respiration
activities_projects.evaluation: Ability to contrast aerobic and anaerobic processes (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Valve Function Model
activities_projects.objective: Understand why blood flows in only one direction
steps: Create a one-way flap inside a tube using rubber
Pour water from one side (flap opens) and then the other (flap closes)
Relate this to the valves in veins and the heart
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Explains the mechanism preventing the backflow of blood
activities_projects.evaluation: Functional demonstration of the one-way valve (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Case Study: Transport in Other Animals
activities_projects.objective: Compare human life processes with other organisms
steps: Research how Amoeba excretes waste without kidneys
Find out how insects breathe through spiracles
Create a comparison table between Humans, Fish, and Insects
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Appreciates the diversity of life processes in the animal kingdom
activities_projects.evaluation: Completeness and accuracy of the comparison table (10 marks)
